title: Willoughbya ternata
intro: Willoughbya ternata, also known as the Australian finger root, is a plant species native to Australia that belongs to the family Costaceae, commonly known as the "finger lily" or "costus" plants. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Willoughbya ternata include costunolide, costunolide acetate, and eudesmane derivatives, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Australian herbal medicine, Willoughbya ternata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a salve or topical cream to treat various health conditions.
